title: Helping retail investors navigate decision fatigue on mobile
summary: This case study explores how I reframed in-app campaign discovery from unstructured feed browsing into an active, evaluation experience, helping investors prioritise and engage with an emphasis on retention.
outcomes: Defined a clear browse → shortlist → decide loop for campaign discovery
Introduced lightweight controls to save, hide, and filter campaigns on mobile
Created a scalable pattern set to support iterative testing and rollouts
+155% increase in campaign follows since release
Launched native app to >25,000 users in UK and Europe

sections.title: Design Challenge
body: Marcus is a retail investor who checks his crowdfunding app a few times a week. Sitting at his kitchen table after work, he scrolls through dozens of live campaigns, with new ones launching regularly.
He wants to make thoughtful decisions, and not miss the next big opportunity.
Over time, the volume starts to blur. Some campaigns catch his eye, but he's not ready to commit. Others feel familiar, but he can't remember whether he's already reviewed them, or why he moved on.
A few days later he opens the app again, unsure what's new and what he's already assessed. Gradually, the experience becomes mentally taxing.
The problem wasn't a lack of information.
It was the absence of continuity, an experience that helped investors stay oriented, filter noise, and build confidence as they returned to the app over time.
sections.leadIn: The ideal browsing experience should help investors:
identifiedItems: Scan quickly and understand what's new, or worth revisiting
Shortlist promising campaigns without forcing a decision
Reduce noise and focus on the opportunities that matter
Return later and pick up where you left off
bodyEnd: To focus the work, I structured it across three core areas:

sections.title: Action Taken
body: I ran a four-week discovery phase with eight investors, combining behavioural analysis, user interviews, usability testing, and a card-sorting exercise on the live campaign page to understand which information investors prioritise when evaluating opportunities.
sections.leadIn: The work focused on three core questions:
identifiedItems: How do investors decide which campaigns to explore?
What information is critical to making an investment decision?
How do they keep track of campaigns they want to return to?
bodyEnd: One insight in particular, "I don't want to see investments I'm not interested in", opened up a new avenue to explore.

Investors didn't want to keep seeing opportunities they'd already ruled out. They didn't want to dig through irrelevant information, and when returning to the app, they wanted clarity on what to look at next.
Discovery wasn't about bookmarking for later, it was about filtering in the moment and progressing with confidence.

sections.title: Results
body: The outcome was a clearer, calmer discovery experience: the feed becomes a place to make progress, not just consume volume.

Investors can now save promising campaigns, remove irrelevant ones, and focus their attention without breaking flow, allowing decisions to build naturally over time rather than forcing them in a single unstructured session.

media.text: ...It just feels better. I can get rid of the ones I'm not into and focus on the good ones.

identifiedItems: A repeatable browse → shortlist → decide loop designed for short sessions
+155% increase in campaign follows since release
Released into native app to UK and Europe, growing user base to >25,000
Contributed to a +0.4 increase in the App Store rating over three months, reaching 4.1/5, reflecting improved user satisfaction.
